info.name: Enterprise ERP API Collection
info.description:
Unified Postman collection for the Enterprise ERP. Features include tenant-scoping headers, automated OAuth token resolution, and complete endpoint maps for all 12 modules.

## Pagination contract

Every `List X` request supports `?page=N&limit=N` (1-based page, limit clamped to [1, 100]; default 15). Tree-shaped endpoints — Folders (eDocuments), CMS Folders, FMS Account chart — are intentionally NOT paginated.

Response envelope:
```json
{
  "data": [ ... ],
  "pagination": { "page": 1, "limit": 15, "total": 312, "totalPages": 21 }
}
```

## Configurable status flows

Every HRM transition endpoint (application pipeline, leave decision, appraisal cycle, vacancy state, employee status, payroll period) reads its allowed states + transitions from the per-tenant `workflow_statuses` table at runtime, NOT from hardcoded enums. Admins manage flows under **IAM › Workflow Statuses**. Module keys: `hrm.application`, `hrm.leave`, `hrm.appraisal`, `hrm.vacancy`, `hrm.employee`, `hrm.payroll_period`. Invalid transitions return **422** with a message like `Cannot transition 'hrm.application' from 'hired' to 'screening'`.

request.description:
Authenticates a tenant user via Passport's Password Grant.

**Response (200):**
```json
{
  "user": { ... },
  "token_type": "Bearer",
  "access_token": "eyJ0eXAi...",
  "refresh_token": "def502...",
  "expires_in": 31536000
}
```

Both `access_token` and `refresh_token` are auto-saved to collection variables.

Exchanges a long-lived `refresh_token` for a fresh `access_token` (and a rotated `refresh_token`). Public endpoint — does NOT require a valid `Authorization` header, since the whole point is to refresh after the access token has expired.

Backend wraps Passport's `/oauth/token` with `grant_type=refresh_token`.

**Response (200):**
```json
{
  "token_type": "Bearer",
  "access_token": "eyJ0eXAi...",
  "refresh_token": "def502...",
  "expires_in": 31536000
}
```

Returns `401` if the refresh token is expired or revoked.
Fetches current logged-in user profile with roles and permissions.
Revokes the active user access token.

item.name: IAM
item.description: Identity, role-based access, and per-tenant workflow configuration.

item.name: Workflow Statuses
item.description: Per-tenant configurable lifecycle status flows. Every HRM status transition (application pipeline, leave decision, appraisal cycle, vacancy state, employee status, payroll period) reads from this table at runtime — domain services call `WorkflowStatusService::validateTransition(module, from, to)` before any state change. Default rows for the 6 HRM modules are populated by the tenant seeder; admins can rename labels, change colors/icons, reorder, or add new statuses without code changes.

query.description: Filter by module key. Omit to list across every module.

request.description:
Lists per-tenant configurable status flows. Filter via `?module=` (e.g. `hrm.application`, `hrm.leave`, `hrm.appraisal`, `hrm.vacancy`, `hrm.employee`, `hrm.payroll_period`).

Domain services (RecruitmentService::transitionApplication, PerformanceService::submit, etc.) validate transitions against this table at runtime.
Returns the distinct list of module keys currently present in `workflow_statuses`. Use this to populate the module dropdown in admin UIs.

Adds a new status into a module's flow.

**Body**
- `module` (required) — dotted module key.
- `key` (required) — lowercase snake_case, unique within the module.
- `label` (required) — human-readable display.
- `color` — one of `primary | success | warning | danger | info | secondary`.
- `icon` — Tabler icon name (e.g. `ti-phone`).
- `sequence` — display order.
- `is_initial` — set true on the bootstrap status (used by Service `initialFor`).
- `is_terminal` — true if no outgoing transitions.
- `allowed_next` — array of `key`s reachable from this state.
Cache is flushed automatically after every mutation.
Soft-deletes the status row. Existing records still referencing it remain unaffected; new transitions to/from it will fail validation.

item.name: eApprovals
item.description:
Generic approval engine consumed by domain modules (HRM Leave, Sales Expense, etc.).

**End-to-end Leave flow:**
1. `Create Leave Approval Workflow` once per tenant with `module=hrm, type=leave` and at least one level.
2. `HRM › Leaves › Submit Leave Request` automatically opens an `ApprovalRequest` — its id is captured into `approval_request_id`.
3. `Process Approval Request` with `action=approved|rejected|sent_back` advances the workflow. On terminal approve/reject, the HRM listener (`SyncLeaveFromApproval`) flips the linked `Leave.status`.

request.description: Creates a workflow keyed on `module=hrm, type=leave`. Once any such workflow exists, `Submit Leave Request` opens an `ApprovalRequest` against its first level. Levels accept `approver_role` (matched against `Role::slug`) and/or `approver_id` (a specific user). Capture id into `workflow_id`.

Records a decision against the current level. `action` ∈ {`approved`, `rejected`, `sent_back`}. On final-level `approved` or any `rejected`, the engine dispatches `ApprovalRequestFinalized`; the HRM listener flips the linked `Leave.status`.
Records a fuel fill-up against a vehicle. `driver_id` is the linked Employee.
Logs hours worked against a Project Task. `hours_worked` accepts decimals.

item.name: HRM
item.description:
Human Resource Management — workforce, leave, and payroll. All routes are tenant-scoped via the `tenant` / `X-Tenant-Handle` headers injected globally and require a Passport bearer token.

Sub-folders are organised by API feature:
- **Employees** — workforce records (CRUD)
- **Departments** — org units
- **Positions** — job titles
- **Leave Types** — annual allowance catalogue
- **Leaves** — request lifecycle + balance
- **Payroll Periods** — period processing
- **Payslips** — pay statement queries

item.name: Employees
item.description: Workforce CRUD. `base_salary` and bank details (`bank_name`, `bank_account_name`, `bank_account_number`) are stored as Laravel-encrypted ciphertext at rest and masked in responses unless the caller holds `hrm.payroll.read`. Account numbers are returned with all but the last 4 digits replaced with `•`.

request.description: Paginated list of employees with department & position eagerly loaded. Optional filters: `status`, `departmentId`, `search`.

Creates an employee. `base_salary` and `bank_*` fields are encrypted at rest and hidden in responses unless the caller holds `hrm.payroll.read`. `bank_account_number` is partially masked even for authorized callers.
Soft-deletes the employee and sets `status=terminated`. Transition validated against `workflow_statuses` (module `hrm.employee`).

item.name: Departments
item.description: Org units used to group employees.

item.name: Leave Types
item.description: Catalogue of leave categories with annual allowance.

item.name: Leaves
item.description:
Leave request lifecycle: submit → approve/reject → withdraw, plus per-employee balance.

**eApprovals wiring** — when a tenant has an `ApprovalWorkflow` with `module=hrm, type=leave`, `Submit Leave Request` automatically opens an `ApprovalRequest` linked to the new leave. The legacy `Approve Leave` / `Reject Leave` endpoints then return **422** with a hint pointing at `POST /api/v1/approval-requests/{id}/process`. When no workflow is configured, the legacy endpoints continue to work as a dev/admin stop-gap.

**Policies** — `LeavePolicy` (registered with `Gate`) enforces `hrm.leave.read/write/delete`. Owners (the linked Employee's user) can view and withdraw their own pending requests without `hrm.leave.delete`.

request.description: Submits a leave request with status=pending. `days` defaults to inclusive span between start/end if omitted. If an `ApprovalWorkflow` exists for `module=hrm, type=leave`, an `ApprovalRequest` is opened atomically and routed to the workflow's first level.

Legacy admin path — approves a pending leave request directly. Transition validated against `workflow_statuses` (module `hrm.leave`). Returns 422 when the employee's remaining balance < days, **or** when an eApprovals request is already active on this leave (clients must use `POST /api/v1/approval-requests/{id}/process` instead). Requires `hrm.leave.write`.

Legacy admin path — rejects a pending leave request directly. Returns 422 when an eApprovals request is already active on this leave (use `POST /api/v1/approval-requests/{id}/process` instead). Requires `hrm.leave.write`.
Returns per leave-type balance sheet: `annualAllowance`, `used`, `remaining` for the current year.

item.name: Payroll Periods
item.description: Create, process (snapshot payslips), and close payroll periods.

request.description: Generates one payslip per active employee using `PayrollService::computeFor` (flat tax 10%, NSSF 4%). Transition draft → processed validated against `workflow_statuses` (module `hrm.payroll_period`).

Locks the period after processing **and** posts the payroll accrual journal entry to FMS. Within one DB transaction:

1. Aggregate gross / tax / nssf across all payslips in the period.
2. Look up four chart-of-accounts codes (`config('payroll.accounts')` — defaults: `EXP-WAGES`, `LIA-TAX`, `LIA-NSSF`, `LIA-WAGES`). Missing codes ⇒ **422** listing what to create in `/api/v1/accounts`.
3. Post a balanced journal: `Dr Wage Expense` / `Cr Tax Payable` / `Cr NSSF Payable` / `Cr Wages Payable`. Reference is `PAYROLL-{period_id}` (unique).
4. Persist `journal_entry_id` + `closed_at` on the period and flip status to `closed`.

Response exposes the new `journalEntryId` + `closedAt` fields. A period without payslips closes without posting (no-op journal). Transition `processed → closed` is also validated against `workflow_statuses` (module `hrm.payroll_period`).

item.name: Payslips
item.description: Pay statement queries. Amounts masked unless owner or `hrm.payroll.read`.

request.description: Amounts are masked unless the caller is the owning employee or holds `hrm.payroll.read`.

item.name: Recruitment
item.description:
Job vacancies + applicant pipeline. Pipeline: applied → screening → assessment → assessment_completed → interview → offer → hired (or rejected/withdrawn).

**Two surfaces:**

- **Candidate-facing (no auth)** — `GET /api/v1/public/job-vacancies`, `GET /api/v1/public/job-vacancies/{id}`, `POST /api/v1/public/applications`. Tenant resolves via `X-Tenant-Handle` like the SPA does, but no Passport bearer is required. Listings are filtered to `status=open`; closed/draft IDs return 404. Submitting against a non-open vacancy → 422.
- **Admin (auth:api + `hrm.recruitment.*`)** — the vacancy lifecycle, full applicant pipeline, and transitions live here.

The public requests appear first below so the candidate-side flow reads top-to-bottom: discover vacancies → submit application.

request.description: Candidate-facing listing. Returns only `status=open` vacancies. No Passport token required — the tenant still resolves through `X-Tenant-Handle`. The first id is captured into `public_job_vacancy_id` for the next call.
Candidate-facing detail. 404 on anything not in `status=open` so candidates can't peek at drafts or closed roles via direct ID.

Candidate-facing submission — no Passport token. Reuses `StoreApplicationRequest` validation. Targets must be `status=open` vacancies; closed/draft IDs surface as 422. `expected_salary` is encrypted at rest and only readable in admin responses with `hrm.recruitment.read`.

body.raw:
{
  "title": "Senior Backend Engineer",
  "description": "Build and operate tenant-isolated services in Laravel + PostgreSQL.",
  "location": "Phnom Penh / Remote",
  "employment_type": "full_time",
  "experience_min_years": 5,
  "experience_max_years": 10,
  "salary_min": 2500.00,
  "salary_max": 4500.00,
  "vacancies_count": 2,
  "status": "draft",
  "department_id": "{{department_id}}",
  "position_id": "{{position_id}}"
}

request.description: Creates a draft vacancy. Call `publish` to open it for applications.
Flips a draft vacancy to status=open and stamps posted_at. Transition validated against `workflow_statuses` (module `hrm.vacancy`).
Closes a vacancy. `reason` must be "closed" or "filled" (both validated as transitions against `workflow_statuses` module `hrm.vacancy`).

Admin path — typed up an application on behalf of a candidate (referrals, walk-ins). For candidate-driven flows use **Public: Submit Application** at the top of this folder instead. Accepts any vacancy status (drafts/closed). `expected_salary` masked in responses unless caller has `hrm.recruitment.read`.

body.raw:
{
  "applicant_name": "Dara Kim",
  "applicant_email": "dara.kim@example.com",
  "applicant_phone": "+85598765432",
  "location": "Phnom Penh, Cambodia",
  "linkedin_url": "https://www.linkedin.com/in/darakim",
  "cover_letter": "Updated cover letter after phone screen.",
  "expected_salary": 3800.00,
  "referrer_employee_id": null,
  "skills": ["PHP", "Laravel", "Vue.js", "PostgreSQL"],
  "notes": "Strong fit for backend role — recommend technical round."
}

request.description: Edit an existing application. All fields optional (`sometimes`/`nullable`). **`job_vacancy_id` and `status` are NOT editable here** — vacancy is locked to preserve funnel attribution; status is moved via the transition endpoint. Requires `hrm.recruitment.write` (policy `ApplicationPolicy::update`).

Advance the pipeline. Allowed transitions are read at runtime from the per-tenant `workflow_statuses` table (module `hrm.application`). Default flow: applied → screening → interview → offer → hired (any stage may → rejected/withdrawn). Invalid transitions return 422.

**Transitioning to `hired` only changes status — it does NOT create an Employee record.** Use the dedicated **Convert to Employee** endpoint for that. Manage the status flow via **IAM › Workflow Statuses**.

Upload a candidate resume ahead of submitting the full application. Returns 201 with `{ path, original_name, size, mime_type }`. The returned `path` should be sent back as `resume_path` in the subsequent **Submit Application** call.

Validation: `file` required, `extensions:pdf,doc,docx` (trusts client extension to avoid octet-stream false rejections), max 10 MB. The service re-checks MIME for defense in depth.

Stored at `storage/tenants/{handle}/hrm/recruitment/application/resume/{uuid}.{ext}` — the `local` disk is auto-rerouted per tenant by FilesystemTenancyBootstrapper.

Requires `hrm.recruitment.write` (policy `ApplicationPolicy::create`).

Promote a `hired` application into an `Employee` record.

- **Idempotent**: repeat calls return the existing linked employee without creating a duplicate.
- **Email dedupe**: if an `Employee` with the same email already exists, that record is reused instead of cloned.
- **Auto employee_id**: new employees get a tenant-wide sequential id in the `TT-NNNN` pattern. **Zero-indexed** — the first auto-issued id on a fresh tenant is `TT-0000`, then `TT-0001`, `TT-0002`, … Generated by `RecruitmentService::generateNextEmployeeId()` from `MAX(numeric_suffix) + 1` scanned across active **and** soft-deleted rows — terminated employees never free their IDs. The pad width is a floor: past `TT-9999` the format widens to `TT-10000`. Prefix and pad are class constants (`EMPLOYEE_ID_PREFIX`, `EMPLOYEE_ID_PAD`).
- **Field mapping**: `department_id`, `position_id` from the vacancy; `expected_salary` → `base_salary`. Employee created with `status='active'`.
- **Side-effect**: stamps `applications.converted_at = now()` so the 7-day revert window can be enforced.
- **Refuses (422)** when the application isn't `hired`.

Requires `hrm.recruitment.write` AND `hrm.employee.write` (policy `ApplicationPolicy::convert`).

Undo a recent hire→employee conversion.

- Renames the linked `Employee`'s `employee_id` to `<original>-REV-<uniqid>` (audited `update()`), then soft-deletes the row.
- Nulls `applications.employee_id` and `applications.converted_at`.
- **Frees the original employee_id for reuse**: the rename takes the original number out of the auto-generator's `^<prefix>-(\d+)$` match set, so the next convert will re-issue it (e.g. revert `TT-0003` → next convert returns `TT-0003`). Contrast with termination, which keeps the ID forever.
- **Refuses (422)** when: not `hired`, no employee linked, `converted_at` missing, OR conversion is older than `RecruitmentService::REVERT_CONVERSION_WINDOW_DAYS` (7 days).

Outside the window, use the standard off-boarding flow via `EmployeeService::terminateEmployee` instead.

Requires `hrm.recruitment.write` AND `hrm.employee.delete` — stricter than `convert` because reverting soft-deletes a workforce record (policy `ApplicationPolicy::revertConversion`).

Soft-delete an application (sets `deleted_at`; preserves the row for audit). Requires `hrm.recruitment.delete` (policy `ApplicationPolicy::delete`).

Note: the in-app UI surfaces this only on rows in `applied` or `screening` status — anything further along has audit/business state that should not be implicitly removed.

Bulk soft-delete applications. Accepts 1–200 UUIDs. Each id is gated through the same `delete` policy as the single-row variant.

Only rows in `applied` or `screening` are eligible; rows further along the pipeline are reported under `skipped`. Returns:
```json
{
  "deleted": int,
  "skipped": string[],
  "missing": string[]
}
```
The UI surfaces this via the multi-select toolbar on `/applications`.

Bulk-convert hired applications to employees. Accepts 1–200 UUIDs. Each id is gated through the `convert` policy.

Each row runs through the same single-row `convertToEmployee` logic — dedupe-by-email guarantee preserved. Returns a structured outcome so the UI can show partial completion:
```json
{
  "converted": int,
  "alreadyLinked": string[],
  "ineligible": string[],
  "missing": string[],
  "errors": [{ "id": uuid, "message": string }]
}
```
- `alreadyLinked`: ids that already had an `employee_id` (no-op).
- `ineligible`: ids that weren't in `hired` status.
- `missing`: ids the tenant doesn't have rows for.
- `errors`: per-row failures (e.g. unique constraint).

Requires `hrm.recruitment.write` AND `hrm.employee.write`.

item.name: Performance
item.description: Appraisal cycle. Lifecycle: draft → submitted → reviewed → closed.

request.description: Creates an appraisal at status=draft. Sensitive fields (rating, notes, goals) are masked in list responses unless caller is the employee, reviewer, or holds `hrm.performance.read`.
Cannot update once status=closed.
draft → submitted, stamps submitted_at. Transition validated against `workflow_statuses` (module `hrm.appraisal`).
submitted → reviewed. Reviewer fills overall_rating + final notes; stamps reviewed_at. Transition validated against `workflow_statuses` (module `hrm.appraisal`).
reviewed → closed. Locks the record from further edits. Transition validated against `workflow_statuses` (module `hrm.appraisal`).
Soft-deletes a draft/submitted/reviewed appraisal. Closed records cannot be deleted (422).

item.name: Quiz Assessment
item.description:
Phase 6 — candidate quiz authoring + sandboxed assessment flow.

**Admin paths** (requires Passport bearer + `hrm.quiz.read|write|delete`):
- `GET/POST /quizzes` — list / create. POST may embed `questions[]` for one-shot authoring.
- `GET/PUT/DELETE /quizzes/{id}` — read / update / archive.
- `POST /quizzes/{id}/questions` — add a single question.
- `POST /applications/{id}/quiz-attempts` — assign a published quiz to a recruitment Application. Returns the raw magic-link token **once** (afterwards only the SHA-256 hash is stored) + a candidate URL.

**Candidate paths** (no Passport — token-authenticated via `?token=...`):
- `GET /candidate/auth?token=...` — landing endpoint, returns attempt + sanitised quiz (no correct answers).
- `POST /candidate/quizzes/{attempt}/start?token=...` — flips invited → in_progress.
- `POST /candidate/quizzes/{attempt}/submit?token=...` — submit answers; QuizService auto-grades and flips the linked Application to `assessment_completed`.

Correct answers live on `quiz_questions.correct_answer` as Laravel-encrypted ciphertext and are exposed in `QuizResource` only to callers with `hrm.quiz.write`. The candidate-facing payload never includes them.

request.description: Creates a quiz with embedded questions. `correct_answer` accepts an array of option keys (single_choice/multiple_choice) or a string (short_text). It is stored as Laravel-encrypted ciphertext and hidden from response unless the caller holds `hrm.quiz.write`. `status` defaults to `draft` — only `published` quizzes can be assigned to candidates.
Appends a single question to an existing quiz.

Issues a magic-link token for a candidate. **Response is the only time the raw token is shown** — afterwards only the SHA-256 hash is stored. Side-effect: advances the linked Application to `assessment`. Quiz must be `published`.
Public endpoint (no Passport). Validates token → returns the attempt + sanitised quiz (questions without correct answers). 404 on bad/expired token.
Idempotent — flips invited → in_progress and stamps `started_at`. Returns 422 if the attempt has already been completed/expired/abandoned.

Answers map question_id → array of option keys (choice questions) or a string (short_text). QuizService auto-grades, persists `score` + `passed` on the attempt, and advances the linked Application to `assessment_completed` (no-op if that status isn't configured).

item.name: Interviewing
item.description:
Phase 7 — interview scheduling + panel feedback.

**Lifecycle** (workflow_statuses module `hrm.interview`): `scheduled → completed | cancelled | no_show`.

**Pivot semantics**: an Interview has an M:N `interviewers` pivot (`interview_interviewer`) of assigned Employees + a separate `interview_feedback` table (one row per interviewer per interview, enforced by a unique pivot). `InterviewPolicy::submitFeedback` short-circuits for any user whose linked Employee is in the assigned pivot — interviewers don't need `hrm.recruitment.write`.

**Side-effects**: scheduling an interview best-effort promotes the linked Application to `interview` via `WorkflowStatusService`; the transition is swallowed if the tenant's `hrm.application` flow doesn't allow it from the current stage. Submit/scheduled feedback never advances the application — HR finalises the hire/reject decision through `/applications/{id}/status`.

**Calendar invites** — `GET /interviews/{id}/invite.ics` streams an RFC 5545 file with all assigned interviewers + the candidate as ATTENDEEs. Direct Google/Outlook OAuth is deferred; download + attach for now.

body.raw:
{
  "application_id": "{{application_id}}",
  "quiz_attempt_id": "{{quiz_attempt_id}}",
  "title": "Technical Panel — Backend Engineer",
  "round": "technical",
  "scheduled_at": "2026-06-02T09:30:00Z",
  "duration_minutes": 75,
  "mode": "video",
  "location": "https://meet.example.com/abc-defg-hij",
  "notes": "Focus on system design + PostgreSQL.",
  "interviewer_ids": [
    "{{employee_id}}"
  ]
}

request.description: Creates an interview and assigns interviewers in one shot. Best-effort moves the Application into the `interview` stage. Requires `hrm.recruitment.write`.
Only `scheduled` interviews can be rescheduled (422 otherwise).
Appends the reason to `notes` and validates the transition against `workflow_statuses` (module `hrm.interview`).
Closes the panel; feedback can still be submitted after this. Final hire/reject decision happens via `/applications/{id}/status`.

body.raw:
{
  "interviewer_id": "{{employee_id}}",
  "rating": 4.5,
  "strengths": "Strong system-design intuition; clean PostgreSQL schema choices.",
  "concerns": "Limited Kafka exposure — would need ramp-up.",
  "recommendation": "hire"
}

request.description: Upsert by `(interview_id, interviewer_id)`. Assigned interviewers can submit without `hrm.recruitment.write` (InterviewPolicy short-circuit).
Aggregate: `averageRating`, `recommendations` tally by key (strong_hire | hire | no_hire | strong_no_hire), `submittedCount`, `pendingCount` (interviewers who haven't submitted yet).
Streams an RFC 5545 calendar invite — `text/calendar`, attachable to email or savable to disk. Includes all assigned interviewers + the candidate as ATTENDEEs.

exec: // Automatically inject/override Multi-Tenant scope and standard Headers on every request without duplicates
